items.headline: City releases youth services master plan and program reports
items.summary: On April 9th, the Council reviewed the Children and Youth Services Master Plan status report along with annual reports on youth empowerment, intervention services, and the Safe Summer Initiative Grant. These reports outline the city's strategic direction and current effectiveness in serving San José's youth population.

items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing postponed
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street has been postponed from April 8 and will be rescheduled for a later date. Residents who were expecting to see this project heard should watch for a new notice with the updated hearing date.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue highway-rail traffic signal project moves forward
items.summary: The City Council approved a third amendment to the consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project on April 7th. This infrastructure project addresses traffic and safety at the intersection of highway and rail corridors on Bascom Avenue.

items.headline: City updates Active Transportation Plan with revised scoring criteria
items.summary: Cupertino is developing an updated Active Transportation Plan (Phase 2) that will guide biking, walking, and transit improvements across the city. The plan includes revised scoring criteria that will determine which projects get prioritized and funded.

items.headline: City Council approves construction project with new contract award
items.summary: The council adopted plans and specifications for a construction project and awarded a contract, though the agenda does not specify which public infrastructure or development this concerns. Residents should review meeting minutes for details on the project scope and location.

items.headline: Milpitas updates federal housing and community development plan
items.summary: The council considered a substantial amendment to the city's 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan, which guides how federal housing and community development funds are spent in Milpitas. This could affect affordable housing priorities and neighborhood improvement projects.

items.headline: Major senior housing complex approved near downtown
items.summary: Trillium senior apartments will add 65 affordable units at 678 E St John St, representing a significant boost to senior housing in San José. At nearly $9M, this is the largest single residential project in this permit batch and addresses growing demand for senior living options.

items.headline: City Council approves emergency interim housing siting policy
items.summary: The Council adopted a new Emergency Interim Housing Siting Policy on April 1st, which will affect how the city places temporary housing for unhoused residents. This policy change could impact where emergency shelters and interim housing sites are located throughout San José.
